title: atomic-skills Plan frontmatter (schemaVersion 0.1/0.2)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of nested `.atomic-skills/projects/<project-id>/<plan-slug>/plan.md` files, with legacy flat `.atomic-skills/plans/<slug>.md` accepted during migration. Current plan writers prefer schemaVersion 0.1; 0.2 remains accepted for explicitly upgraded files. Mirrors aideck/src/schemas/project-status.ts:Plan. The `narrative` field of the TypeScript Plan interface lives in the markdown body, NOT frontmatter — so it is not declared here.

stateIntegrityHardening.description: Forward-only opt-in for strict project/plan/phase identity, terminality and uniqueness cross-validation. Legacy plans remain readable until migrated and explicitly hardened.

successorBarriers.description: Descriptor-only activations that must revalidate a historical receipt and a prerequisite phase close commit through scripts/materialize-state.js.

closedAtHardening.description: F4/T-003: opts this plan into the closedAt forward-only hard-gate. Written ONCE by scripts/harden-closedat.js. grandfatheredTaskIds = the done-without-closedAt tasks alive at the flip instant (exempt); every later done task must record closedAt. Never implies a retroactive closedAt (P3).

planActive.description: Derived focus marker (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): true iff status:'active'. Carried by the `phases` dataSource onto each phase row so the Home timeline scopes to active plans (the dashboard can't join). Never hand-authored.

planTitle.description: Derived denormalization (scripts/reconcile-focus.js): a copy of `title`, carried by the `phases` dataSource onto each phase row so a repeat-grouped widget can label the group with the human plan title instead of the slug (the dashboard can't join). Never hand-authored.

spawnedFrom.description: Fork child→parent origin edge (rung 7.5): points at the parent plan + the anchor phase it was extracted from. Bidirectional with the parent phase's `spawnedPlans`. Additive (distinct from `supersedes`, which is replacement): the parent consumes the child's result. Mirrors aideck spawnedFromSchema and the inline field migrated from legacy links.json. Durable edge fields are identity + mode only; fork rationale `context`/`provenance` is not persisted on this edge. `mode`: pause = parent waits for the child; parallel = both active, each in its own worktree.

dependsOnPlans.description: Operational plan dependency edges persisted on the blocked plan. Each entry points at a prerequisite plan in the same project. This is separate from `spawnedFrom` / `phases[].spawnedPlans`, which describe origin only.

archived.description: `archived` prerequisites block by default; `resolved` records an explicit decision that archiving satisfied this edge.

summary.description: Concise one-line human summary of what this phase does — distinct from the longer, technical `goal`. Authored + user-validated when phases are generated (post-decompose annotation; decompose.js format stays frozen per R-ORCH-10). Shown on the dashboard Home timeline. Helps a dev recall each phase + validates the AI's interpretation.

businessIntent.description: F0/T-001 (phase-materialization): the load-bearing business spine — value/workflow/rules/outOfScope/doneWhen — authored + user-validated when the phase is materialized (D1 strong-lazy), persisted on both the plan phase descriptor and the initiative file (F-001), and surfaced for the materialize gate (D4). Optional + additive (mirrors `summary`): an absent block means a not-yet-activated/legacy surface. `derived[]` captures open questions + tentative answers (never gated). scripts/find-missing-business-intent.js (T-003) hard-blocks a materialized phase missing any spine field.

spawnedPlans.description: Fork parent→child origin edge (rung 7.5): child plan slugs forked out of THIS phase. Bidirectional with each child's top-level `spawnedFrom`. Additive — phases without forks omit it. Mirrors aideck phaseDescriptorSchema.spawnedPlans and the inline field migrated from legacy links.json. Durable edge fields are child slugs only; fork rationale `context`/`provenance` is not persisted on this edge.

reviewGate.description: 0.2 (G2 phase-review gate): the outcome of phase-done's review-code gate for THIS phase, written before the phase is set 'done' (project-transitions.md phase-done step 6/8). Shape only here; the cross-field honesty conditional (done + status:passed ⟹ `at` sha present; done + status:skipped ⟹ `reason` present) is enforced by scripts/validate-state.js GATE-R3, NOT the schema — same reason GATE-R2 lives in JS (keeps the aiDeck TS mirror additive-optional). An absent block on a legacy 'done' phase is tolerated (GATE-R2-consistent); `verify` WARNs on it.

status.description: passed = review-code ran and blocker/critical findings were resolved; skipped = --skip-review with a recorded reason.

mode.description: which review mode ran (a destructive phase runs 'both' per G5).

at.description: the commit sha the review concluded against (post-fix HEAD). Required-when-passed by GATE-R3.

reviewFile.description: path to the persisted review file, when one was written (e.g. .atomic-skills/reviews/<…>.md).

reason.description: why review was skipped. Required-when-skipped by GATE-R3.

allOf.description: Phases inserted mid-plan (with provenance) must articulate WHY via context. Original-materialization phases live without context — their narrative is the plan body.
